title: Tesla finally launches a bike — but it’s not what fans wanted
summary:
Tesla fans have spent years begging the company to build an e-bike. Tesla just launched a bike — and it has no motor, no pedals, and it’s for toddlers.

The new “Balance Bike for Kids” sells for $225 on Tesla’s online shop, and it’s aimed at children aged 2 to 5.

title: The BMW iX4 looks more like a grand tourer than an SUV [Images]
summary: The 2027 iX4 is set to join BMW’s electric vehicle lineup soon, bringing a unique new look to the family. Ahead of its debut, the 2027 BMW iX4 was spotted on the road in Germany.

title: Tesla (TSLA) shareholders are begging Musk to explain missed goals
summary:
Tesla shareholders have flooded the company’s Q2 2026 earnings Q&A portal with questions, and the most-upvoted ones tell a bleak story: retail investors are now demanding that Elon Musk explain why the company keeps missing its own short-term goals.

The tone has shifted from asking about Tesla’s grand vision to asking why almost none of it is showing up on schedule — on robotaxi, “Full Self-Driving,” and Optimus.

title: Kia EV2 Long Range goes on sale with 281 miles of range, plus GT and Black-Line trims
summary: The Kia EV2 is now available with a larger battery providing up to 281 miles of driving range, plus a few new trim options.

title: Xpeng’s new L03 undercuts Tesla Model Y by up to $10,000 in Europe
summary:
Xpeng launched its new L03 electric SUV-coupe in Europe this week with a starting price of €34,990 — undercutting the Tesla Model Y by roughly €3,400 in Germany and by around $10,000 in Norway.

The pricing puts a well-equipped 4.65-meter electric SUV directly under Tesla’s best-seller in its highest-volume segment, and deliveries start in the fourth quarter.

title: Segway Max G3 e-scooter at $1,000 2026 low, EcoFlow dual-bundle power station flash sale, Tapo solar security camera 3-pack low, more
summary:
Leading today’s Green Deals are a special promotion on Segway’s Max G3 Electric Scooter at a $1,000 2026 low for members (sign-up is free). Right behind it, we have an EcoFlow flash sale that is offering double bundles of power stations, expansion batteries, and more – all starting from $879. There’s also the Tapo SolarCam 1080p Outdoor Wireless Security Camera 3-pack at its $120 low that was last seen during Prime Day, as well as EGO’s 56V 26-inch Cordless Electric Hedge Trimmer with a 2.5Ah battery at a new $195 Amazon low, and some additional deals waiting for you below. As always, you can find all the hangover deals from this week down at the bottom of the page, like yesterday’s Wellbots Day 13th Anniversary exclusive savings on collections of EcoFlow and Jackery power stations that ends tonight, and more.

Head below for other New Green Deals we’ve found today and, of course, Electrek’s best EV buying and leasing deals. Also, check out the new Electrek Tesla Shop for the best deals on Tesla accessories.

title: Honda is officially pulling the plug on its only EV in the US
summary: The Honda Prologue is still among America’s top 10 best-selling electric vehicles, but it won’t be for much longer.

title: Volvo delivers first EX60 EVs, a ‘game changer’ with 500+ miles range and fast charging
summary: The EX60 is a true “game changer” for the Volvo brand, with the first customer deliveries now underway.

title: Chip Motors unveils $15,000 electric ‘life utility vehicle’
summary:
Miami-based startup Chip Motors has unveiled Chip, an electric “life utility vehicle” that starts at $15,000 and tops out at 25 mph.

The boxy, open-air four-seater is a low-speed vehicle aimed at short daily trips, and it lands in one of the fastest-growing corners of the EV market.

title: XPeng L03 ships Google Maps Auto SDK — a first for an APAC automaker
summary:
XPeng used the global launch of its new L03 in Munich to announce that the electric SUV coupe is the first vehicle from any Asia-Pacific automaker to ship with Google Maps’ Auto SDK built in.

The integration replaces XPeng’s overseas navigation stack with Google Maps data and, more importantly, feeds map data into its NGP and XPILOT ASSIST driver-assistance systems outside China.

title: Launch Mode Actually Makes The 2026 Tesla Model Y Performance Slower
summary: The newest Model Y Performance is faster to 60 mph when not using launch mode. What gives?

title: Honda Just Axed Its Last EV In The U.S.
summary: Say goodbye to the Honda Prologue. With that and the Acura ZDX both canceled, Honda is left without an electric offering in America.

title: Americans Can't Have These Affordable Chinese EVs. But They're Coming To Canada
summary: China's Dongfeng Motor showed off a gaggle of cars in Montreal, with plans to sell its first two starting in 2027.

title: Volvo Could Bring An Electric Sedan And Wagon To U.S. By 2028: Report
summary: Volvo is reportedly considering bringing a sedan and wagon back to the U.S. as EVs, after discontinuing its gas-powered models.

title: EV Startup Reveals $15,000 25-MPH Car That You'll Never Have To Park
summary: Chip Motors is a new EV startup betting that Americans are willing to go smaller—and pay up to have someone else do the parking.

title: Someone Is Already Trying To Flip One Of The Last Tesla Model S's Built. The Price Is... Ambitious
summary: Is owning the last of the breed worth an extra $100,000? One enterprising dealer is trying to find out.

title: This Tesla Model 3 Battery Chemistry Ages Better Than The Rest
summary: Four Model 3 battery variants were compared after 62,000 miles, and the CATL LFP pack retained the most of its initial capacity.

title: America Lost Over 111,000 Jobs Due To Trump's Clean Energy Pullback
summary: After Trump reversed Biden-era incentives, 223 clean-energy programs were ended, resulting in $82.9 billion in investments lost.

title: 'Completely False': Lucid Denies Report That It's Weighing Bankruptcy After Shares Crash
summary: A report that Lucid was considering bankruptcy triggered a massive selloff and an official denial from the automaker.

title: Nearly 10,000 EVs Were Tested For Battery Degradation. These 2 Models Won Out.
summary: A Swedish analysis of nearly 10,000 battery tests found that two EVs retained more than 97% of their capacity after 62,000 miles.
